title: Portal - Digital Asset Management
sub: Designed a centralized digital asset management platform that enables sales teams to organize, govern, collaborate, and securely share sales collateral from a single workspace, reducing document friction, improving collaboration, and accelerating deal closures.
content:
Overview

Portal is a Digital Asset Management (DAM) platform designed to simplify document management throughout the sales lifecycle. Sales teams regularly collaborate with marketing, legal, and product teams to create presentations, proposals, contracts, and supporting collateral. As organizations grow, these documents often become scattered across emails, cloud storage, and collaboration tools, making it difficult to locate the latest version. Portal centralizes these assets into a single workspace where teams can organize, govern, collaborate on, and securely share sales content without disrupting the sales process.

The Problem

Sales representatives were spending valuable time searching for documents instead of engaging with prospects. Multiple versions of the same file created confusion, while disconnected approval processes slowed down deal progression. Sharing files required downloading documents from one platform and uploading them into another, creating repetitive work, increasing the risk of human error, and reducing overall productivity.

The Solution

Portal combines digital asset management, document governance, collaboration, and secure document sharing into one unified platform. Teams can manage document lifecycles, collaborate across departments, maintain version history, and publish customer-ready documents through secure Deal Rooms. An analytics dashboard also provides visibility into document activity and deal progress, enabling sales teams to monitor performance throughout the sales pipeline.

Design Process

Brief

The objective was to design a centralized platform that simplifies sales document management, reduces operational friction, and improves collaboration between cross-functional teams while accelerating the sales cycle.

Define

The primary goals were to centralize document management, eliminate version conflicts, simplify governance workflows, improve collaboration, enable secure document sharing, and provide meaningful insights into document performance. The project followed a structured design process consisting of research, ideation, wireframing, visual design, prototyping, and usability testing using Figma, Notion, and Jira.

Ideation

The project began by creating a Sales Development Representative persona to better understand user goals, frustrations, and day-to-day workflows. This user-centered approach ensured every design decision addressed real user needs. User journey mapping highlighted friction throughout the document preparation and approval process, while affinity mapping organized research findings into recurring themes such as poor document discoverability, collaboration delays, and version confusion. A Dollar Test helped prioritize features by asking users to allocate value across proposed solutions, revealing that AI-powered search, version control, and secure document sharing were the most valuable capabilities.

Key Features

AI Document Classifier

An intelligent document classifier automatically generates metadata and relevant tags based on document content, making assets easier to organize, search, and retrieve while significantly reducing manual effort.

Document Version Control

Every document maintains a complete revision history, allowing teams to identify the latest approved version, compare revisions, and restore previous iterations when required. This eliminates uncertainty and ensures customers always receive accurate documentation.

Governance Workflow

Built-in approval workflows enable legal, marketing, and management teams to review documents, approve changes, or request revisions directly within the platform. This improves accountability while maintaining compliance and brand consistency.

Deal Room

Deal Rooms allow sales representatives to create secure private links where prospects can access presentations, contracts, proposals, and supporting documents. This removes the need to repeatedly download and upload files across different platforms, reducing workflow interruptions and improving the customer experience.

Design

A UI audit of existing workflows revealed inconsistencies in navigation, spacing, typography, and interaction patterns. To address these issues, a reusable design toolkit was created containing standardized components, typography, color tokens, icons, buttons, and interaction states. Information architecture was reorganized around user tasks rather than system functions, while low-fidelity wireframes validated navigation patterns before progressing into high-fidelity interface designs. The resulting design system improved consistency across design, development, and marketing teams while making future product expansion significantly easier.

Outcome

Portal reduced friction across the entire document lifecycle by centralizing document management into a single platform. Sales teams could retrieve files more quickly, collaborate more efficiently, eliminate version conflicts, and securely deliver customer-ready documents without switching between multiple applications. The streamlined workflow allowed teams to spend more time building customer relationships and less time managing files.

User Feedback

“The interface feels incredibly organized and intuitive. Finding documents now takes seconds instead of minutes, and having approvals, version history, and customer sharing all in one place has dramatically improved how our sales team collaborates.” — Product Manager, MathCo

What I Learned

This project reinforced how critical quick access to accurate information is for maintaining momentum throughout the sales pipeline. Even small delays caused by searching for files or waiting for approvals can slow down deal progression. I also learned that consolidating multiple workflows into a single platform significantly reduces cognitive load and improves efficiency, allowing users to focus on customer relationships rather than administrative tasks. Designing for enterprise collaboration also strengthened my understanding of balancing business requirements with user needs while creating scalable product experiences.

Missed Opportunities

Although the initial project scope included developing a complete visual identity with custom iconography, typography, and a dedicated brand asset library, these initiatives were deferred because of timeline constraints. Expanding Portal into a fully branded design language remains an exciting opportunity for future iterations.

Moving Forward

As Portal continues to onboard new customers, future iterations will leverage anonymous usage analytics and customer feedback to identify usability improvements, introduce additional workflow automation, and further enhance the overall experience while supporting increasingly complex enterprise sales operations.

title: Forma — Material Intelligence
sub: An AI-assisted material selection tool for sustainable architecture
content:
Background

Architecture firms spend an average of 340 hours per project on material research and specification. Forma reduces this to under 40 hours while simultaneously improving sustainability outcomes and cost predictability.

Core Challenge

Material selection involves thousands of interdependent variables: structural performance, embodied carbon, cost trajectories, lead times, aesthetic coherence, and regulatory compliance. No single expert can hold all of this in their head. Forma makes it possible.

Intelligence Layer

The underlying model was trained on 2.3 million material specifications, 180,000 completed projects, and real-time pricing from 600 suppliers. It understands context — a coastal building faces different material constraints than an inland one, even if the programs are identical.

Interface Design

Rather than surfacing a list of options, Forma surfaces a field of possibilities — a spatial canvas where materials cluster by compatibility, sustainability profile, and cost band. Architects explore by zooming, filtering, and comparing, not by navigating menus.

Collaboration Features

A shared project space allows structural engineers, interior designers, and clients to annotate the material field together. Decisions are logged with reasoning, creating a searchable specification history that survives personnel changes.

Impact

Used by 340 architecture firms in 28 countries. Average embodied carbon reduction: 23% compared to traditional selection methods. Average specification time reduction: 88%.
